description:
Advances: specled.mix_tasks, specled.coverage_capture

Decision 2: task KEEPS its name; behavior change goes in 0.4.0 release notes. Default mode: drop install_formatter/0, drop async forcing (ExUnit.configure(async: false) never actually serialized async modules — confirmed overclaim), drop the post-run stderr regex scan; run 'mix test --cover --export-coverage specled'; ingest in an after block guarded by coverdata existence; exit-code passthrough for red suites; non-zero exit on ingestion refusal or empty coverage. Include red-suite passthrough test.

## Orchestrator addenda (admission, epic @ 81006e9)

Landed upstream on your base — build on these, do not re-implement: Store v2 (write_v2/read_v2/read_status), Aggregate.ingest/2 + mix spec.cover.ingest (155.2), formatter disarm-by-default + :specled_ex :spec_cover_run arming seam + line-level armed snapshots (155.4 — the task already carries the minimal arming put_env lines; your rework subsumes them). Maintainer decision 2 (binding): the task KEEPS the name mix spec.cover.test.

Default mode becomes: run 'mix test --cover --export-coverage specled' with NO formatter install and NO ExUnit.configure(async: false) and NO post-run stderr regex scan; then ingest the exported coverdata via Aggregate.ingest/2 into the v2 artifact path, guarded by coverdata existence; suite exit code passes through; ingestion refusal/empty coverage exits non-zero with the refusal reason. Keep the no-@requirements/app.config child-BEAM-compatible pattern (see 155.2's ingest task and completion notes).

--per-test mode (opt-in flag): serialized flow (the old run shape), arms the formatter via the :specled_ex seam, keeps the async file scan, exits non-zero on async contamination unless --allow-async (which degrades: async-flagged records, degraded envelope/status). The capture ENGINE under this lane is rebuilt in specled_-155.5 — do not build Snapshot/diffing here; drive the existing (now honest) formatter.

KNOWN TENSION: test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs pins the current default (serialized formatter run). Your default-mode change legitimately rewrites those expectations — the new honest contract is the test target. Keep a test lane for --per-test behavior.

If branch_guard_missing_decision_update fires (default-mode change is cross-cutting), the epic precedent is a Spec-Drift <code>=info trailer claiming the epic's already-decided design (155.2's was verify-ratified); the durable ADR (aggregate_first_spec_coverage) is specled_-155.8's deliverable — do NOT write it here.

Spec co-change is authorized and required when the gate demands it: co-edit only the .spec/specs sections your diff directly invalidates (mix_tasks.spec.md and coverage_capture.spec.md sections about the task's default/serialized behavior); .spec/realization_hashes.json is tool-managed. A green gate outranks Out of Scope.

LESSON (from specled_-155.1): append new code below existing api_boundary-bound functions — line shifts above them trigger spurious realization drift (specled_-wp7).

## Allowed touches

- lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex
- test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md, .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md (only sections your diff invalidates + stub flips)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex, lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex (T5 owns further engine work)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/store.ex, lib/specled_ex/coverage/aggregate.ex, lib/specled_ex/coverage/mfa_key.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.ingest.ex (CALL, never edit)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex, lib/specled_ex/review/** (155.6/.7, in flight)
- docs/**

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No new external deps
- No Snapshot/delta engine work (T5)
- No renaming the task (decision 2)
- Never :cover.reset

## Verification

- Default child-BEAM fixture run: task executes plain 'mix test --cover --export-coverage specled' (no formatter registered, async config untouched), ingests to a v2 :aggregate envelope at the artifact path, exit 0, Store.read_status {:ok, stats}
- Red suite: task exit code non-zero (passthrough), no bogus artifact left behind
- Empty coverage: non-zero exit naming the refusal; Store.read_status {:refused, ...}
- --per-test: formatter armed via :specled_ex seam and produces per-test records (serialized); async contamination → non-zero exit; --allow-async → degraded-but-successful run
- mix help spec.cover.test exit 0 (name kept)
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors, targeted tests, full mix spec.check all green

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.3/codex-impl-1/log

Commit: 9607c97b333edd2c8a2d305968f854077dfb35ca
Files touched: lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs, .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md
Tests added: Mix.Tasks.Spec.Cover.TestTest "mix help spec.cover.test exits 0 and keeps the task name", "green suite: plain mix test --cover --export-coverage, no formatter, ingests aggregate envelope", "red suite: exit code passes through and real (non-placeholder) coverage still ingests", "empty coverage: non-zero exit naming the refusal, status reports :refused", "runs serialized, arms the formatter, writes per_test.coverdata", "async contamination without --allow-async exits non-zero naming the file", "--allow-async degrades instead of failing: exits 0 with a warning"
Verification stubs flipped: none (all new coverage_capture.spec.md requirement ids were authored directly as execute: true tagged_tests; no pre-existing execute: false stub existed for this ticket's scope)
Verification tier: change-scoped — ran test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_ingest_test.exs, and test/specled_ex/coverage/** together (59 tests, 0 failures) with --include integration; full `mix spec.check` (pass, errors=0, warnings=0); mix compile --warnings-as-errors and MIX_ENV=test mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix format --check-formatted clean. Full-system proof (whole `mix test` suite) deferred to the merge gate / PR CI per skill instructions.
Final-tier runs: 1

Summary: Reworked `mix spec.cover.test` so its default mode runs plain `mix test --cover --export-coverage specled` (no formatter, no async-forcing) and ingests the exported coverdata — guarded by its existence — via `SpecLedEx.Coverage.Aggregate.ingest/2` into a v2 envelope, passing the wrapped suite's exit code through and forcing non-zero on ingestion refusal or empty coverage. The old serialized per-test capture moved behind an opt-in `--per-test` flag, which now hard-fails on `async: true` contamination unless `--allow-async` degrades it to a warning-only run; the task keeps its name per Decision 2. Co-changed `.spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md` (rescoped `serialized_run` to `--per-test`, added five new requirement/scenario ids for the default-mode and async-contamination behaviors) to keep `mix spec.check` green — empirically verified `mix test --cover --export-coverage` still exports coverdata on a failing suite and that a wrapping `after` block runs normally (no VM halt), which the design relies on for exit-code passthrough.

Note: `--allow-async`'s "degraded" run is a stderr warning + exit 0 only (no schema-level degraded marking in the per-test v1 record artifact, since the per-test capture engine/schema itself is explicitly Out of Scope here — reserved for specled_-155.5, which already exists and owns the --per-test engine rebuild). No new follow-up ticket created; deferring to the existing 155.5.

title: T3: Rework mix spec.cover.test default mode (aggregate, no formatter)
